summary: 4-step Fibonacci on signed i64 with tail-recursive 4-window slide extracted via `partial_fixpoint`; base cases at 0/1/2/3 closed by `native_decide` unit pins, recurrence on safe range [4,50] proved by bundling ALL 51 Rust↔oracle equivalences as a SINGLE bounded-universal `native_decide` (`∀ k ≤ 50, fib4 (Int64.ofNat k) = RustM.ok (Int64.ofInt (fib4_oracle k))`) — amortising one native compile across all cases instead of per-case enumeration (a prior 47-arm `omega`-disjunction approach exceeded the build timeout; the file's own comment records this as the motivating insight). Paired with an iteratively-defined Nat→Int oracle `fib4_oracle` (O(n) eval; the naive 4-way recursion is O(4^n) and intractable for `native_decide`), a bounded-universal `int64_ofNat_sub` lemma discharging `(Int64.ofNat (m+4) - j : i64) = Int64.ofNat (m+4 - j)` for j ∈ {1,..,4} in one `native_decide`, and `Int64.ofInt_eq_of_toInt_eq` lifting `n : i64` with `n.toInt ∈ [4,50]` to `Int64.ofNat k` (signed analog of `clever_038_prime_fib`'s `UInt64.ofNat_eq_of_toNat_eq` case-split). Recurrence stated existentially over 5 witnesses, with the `.toInt`-additive identity closed via a per-witness `fib4_oracle_toInt_ofInt` round-trip backed by a bounded-universal `fib4_oracle_bounded` (every value fits in i64 for k ≤ 53). Successor pattern to the per-case enumeration shape in clever_038_prime_fib for proofs where the recurrence couples multiple indices.
